task.title: Supporting error feedback for other languages
task.shortdesc: The product provides error feedback support for COBOL and PL/I, but you can set properties for supporting error feedback for other languages, such as Assembler.

keywords.indexterm: error feedbackclient setupproperties for other languages

steps.stepsection: To set up properties to support error feedback for other languages, such as Assembler, do these steps:

step.cmd: From a properties window, open the language-specific Compile or Assemble Step Options window.
step.info: To open this window, do the following steps:

substep.cmd: From the Assembler Settings, COBOL Settings, or PL/I Settings page of the properties window, select the Procedures and Steps tab.
Select the procedure name for the compilation or assemble step.
To enable the procedure, click Enable procedure.
substep.info: A procedure must be enabled before you can edit the step.

substep.cmd: Click Edit step to open the Compile or Assemble Step Options window.

step.cmd: Select the Support Error Feedback check box.
step.stepresult: The Data Set Qualifier for Compiler Errors field is activated.

step.cmd: Specify the error feedback qualifier that is used by to generate the SYSXMLSD DD card in the JCL.
step.info: Your error feedback program writes the error feedback XML file to the data set specified by the SYSXMLSD Card. If your error feedback program writes the XML file in UTF-8, make sure the qualifier name that is specified includes ERRWDZ. transfers files with ERRWDZ as a qualifier in binary format to avoid any code page translations. To handle the file transfer, a default mapping for **ERRWDZ is defined in the z/OS file system mappings.
